I don't really understand the meaning of "resolution factor", in particular how it provides additional information not already encoded into width×height/dpi.

EDIT: I understand now, it can be useful to have the virtual display DPI not correlated to the client resolution.
author: rom1v

body:
> I don't really understand the meaning of "resolution factor", in particular how it provides additional information not already encoded into width×height/dpi.

(no resolution) "resizable" factor is to multiply the real window size to send to the virtual display resolution
ex: window size 100x50 with factor 2, will resize the virtual display to 200x100
this will avoid small screen resolution (as same window size)
the width×height/dpi is not applicable as the window will resize the display, you may indicate the initial resolution but after resizing the scrcpy window will resize the display

the autofactor (:r with out N factor) will calculate the factor with default window size and default resolution

body:
The latest available through `sdkmanager` is
platforms;android-36.1 - Android SDK Platform 36.1
and needs to be specified as `36.1`, because it gets installed in
`/usr/local/share/android-commandlinetools/platforms/android-36.1/`

Otherwise:
```
ANDROID_PLATFORM=36 ANDROID_BUILD_TOOLS=36.1.0 ./server/build_without_gradle.sh 
Platform: android-36
Build-tools: 36.1.0
Build dir: /Users/Shared/src/scm/android/scrcpy/build_manual
Generating java from aidl...
ERROR: /usr/local/share/android-commandlinetools/platforms/android-36/framework.aidl:
  Error while opening file for parsing
```

With this patch (and 36.1):
```
ANDROID_PLATFORM=36.1 ANDROID_BUILD_TOOLS=36.1.0 ./server/build_without_gradle.sh 
Platform: android-36.1
Build-tools: 36.1.0
Build dir: /Users/Shared/src/scm/android/scrcpy/build_manual
Generating java from aidl...
Compiling java sources...
Dexing...
Server generated in /Users/Shared/src/scm/android/scrcpy/build_manual/scrcpy-server
```

body:
It is working and somewhat stable on high latency. Tested from a laptop running linux and an android 16 device.

The new arguments are like this:
```
<     --client-audio-source=source
<         Inject audio into the device microphone from a device or file.
<         The source can be:
<           - A device name (e.g., "Microphone", "default")
<           - A file path prefixed with "file://" (e.g.,
<         "file:///path/to/audio.mp3")
<         Supported file formats: MP3, OGG, WAV, FLAC, etc.
< 

<     --list-audio-sources
<         List available audio input sources on the client computer.

```


This is not enabled by default so you have to explicitly add the argument, e.g
`scrcpy --client-audio-source default`

body:
## Changes Summary This PR implements client-side microphone audio forwarding to Android devices, allowing users to inject audio from their computer's microphone (or audio files) into the Android device. The feature includes a new audio decoder (Opus format), audio injector using Android's AudioPolicy APIs, command-line options for audio source selection, and a utility to list available audio sources. **Type:** feature **Components Affected:** Client-side audio capture (C), Server-side audio injection (Java), Command-line interface, Socket management for microphone stream, Audio decoding pipeline, Options/configuration system
Files Changed | File | Summary | Change | Impact | |:-----|:--------|:------:|:------:| | `app/src/client_audio.c` | New module implementing client-side microphone audio capture using FFmpeg/libav libraries, with support for device enumeration and file-based audio sources. | ➕ | 🔴 | | `app/src/client_audio.h` | Header file defining microphone parameters structure and public API for listing audio sources and running the microphone capture thread. | ➕ | 🔴 | | `app/src/cli.c` | Added command-line options `--client-audio-source` and `--list-audio-sources` to support microphone forwarding feature. | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `app/src/cli.h` | Added `list_audio_sources` field to scrcpy_cli_args structure. | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/src/options.h` | Added `client_audio_source` field to scrcpy_options structure for storing the audio source configuration. | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/src/options.c` | Updated options parsing to handle client_audio_source field. | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/src/main.c` | Added initialization of avdevice for microphone support and handling of `--list-audio-sources` flag; moved avdevice registration outside of HAVE_V4L2 ifdef. | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `app/src/server.c` | Added client_mic_socket management, connection setup, TCP_NODELAY optimization, and proper cleanup for client audio streaming. | ✏️ | 🔴 | | `app/src/server.h` | Added client_audio boolean flag to server_params and client_mic_socket field to server structure. | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `app/src/scrcpy.c` | Added microphone thread creation and management, passing client_audio_source to the microphone capture module. | ✏️ | 🔴 | | `app/meson.build` | Added client_audio.c source file to the build configuration. | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/tests/test_cli.c` | Updated test structures to initialize list_audio_sources field in all test cases. | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `...n/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/AudioInjector.java` | New Java class implementing audio injection into Android device microphone using AudioPolicy and reflection APIs, supporting various microphone input modes. | ➕ | 🔴 | | `...rc/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/Options.java` | Added client_audio boolean option and getter method for configuration. | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `...src/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/Server.java` | Integrated audio decoder and injector into main server loop; retrieves client audio socket and processes audio stream through decoder and injector pipeline. | ✏️ | 🔴 | | `...a/com/genymobile/scrcpy/audio/AudioDecoder.java` | New audio decoder handling Opus format decompression, configuring MediaCodec with proper CSD (codec-specific data) headers. | ➕ | 🔴 | | `...genymobile/scrcpy/device/DesktopConnection.java` | Added clientAudioSocket connection handling, accepting/connecting to microphone socket, and providing accessor method. | ✏️ | 🔴 |
Architecture Impact - **New Patterns:** Audio streaming pipeline (client capture → encoding → transmission → decoding → injection), Thread-based async processing (microphone capture runs in separate thread), Socket-based IPC for audio stream transport, Android reflection-based API access (AudioPolicy) - **Dependencies:** added: libavdevice for audio device enumeration and capture, added: libswresample for audio format conversion, added: Android MediaCodec for audio decoding (server-side) - **Coupling:** New coupling between main scrcpy flow and microphone thread; audio socket now treated as peer to video/audio/control sockets in server initialization. Client audio processing is tightly integrated into Server.java main loop. - **Breaking Changes:** Changed validation logic: `--no-video --no-audio --no-control` without microphone is now invalid (was previously invalid, now includes `--no-client-audio` in the check), avdevice registration now unconditional (moved from HAVE_V4L2 conditional) - may have minor platform impact
**Risk Areas:** Android AudioPolicy API use via reflection: Fragile across Android versions, relies on private APIs that may change or be restricted on newer Android versions, Audio format/codec assumptions: Hardcoded Opus format and stereo 48kHz configuration may not work universally; limited flexibility for different audio formats, Buffer management: PipedInputStream/PipedOutputStream with 500KB buffer - potential blocking/deadlock if producer/consumer rates mismatch, Error handling in audio decoder: Limited error recovery in AudioDecoder.start() and audio injection pipeline - exceptions logged but may silently fail, Platform-specific audio capture: Uses different FFmpeg input formats (WASAPI/dshow on Windows, avfoundation on macOS, ALSA on Linux) - testing burden across platforms, Memory/resource lifecycle: Multiple concurrent streams (video, audio, microphone) - potential for resource exhaustion with long-running sessions, Socket connection ordering: Complex socket management logic in server.c for client_audio_socket - subtle bugs possible in connection establishment or cleanup
Suggestions - Add comprehensive error handling and recovery in the audio decoder - currently exceptions may silently fail the audio injection - Consider making audio format/codec configurable rather than hardcoding Opus stereo 48kHz - Add logging/debugging facilities for audio injection pipeline to help diagnose issues across different Android versions - Consider version detection for Android AudioPolicy APIs to provide graceful fallback on older devices - Add integration tests for the microphone forwarding feature with different audio sources and formats - Document the private API dependency and version compatibility matrix for AudioPolicy - Review buffer sizes (500KB PipedInputStream) under various load conditions to ensure no deadlocks - Consider adding audio format conversion capabilities if supporting multiple formats is desired
Full review in progress... | Powered by diffray

body: Well, I don't really like these tools, but I must admit it actually found a real bug *outside* this PR, see https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6439#issuecomment-3694956690, at the bottom: "1 issue(s) outside PR diff (click to expand)". Commit f9960e959fa7b46cc1ed6b15115fe2958724766d is the culprit. Any static analyzer should give a warning, though, AI is not needed for that AFAIK.

author: rom1v

body:
> Well, I don't really like these tools, but I must admit it actually found a real bug outside this PR

AI tools can be great for reviews, but I don't want them to be intrusive. I don't want an unsolicited bot posting a ton of AI-generated review comments.

What could be useful is a tool or website where I can push a branch or a PR and get an explanation of possible issues (like a powerful static analyzer), without adding noise to the PR thread.
author: rom1v

body:
@JunaidQrysh I can confirm that `scrcpy --audio-source=voice-call-downlink --client-audio-source default` works best. The other person being called can hear me and I can hear them. I have not tested with recording. Are you recording with the device running scrcpy or the other one on the call?

In reality, `--audio-source=voice-call-uplink --client-audio-source <xxx>` do not make sense / are useless to be with each other because even if it worked, they would just make the pc/laptop replay the audio from it's own microphone. I think it's best to simply disallow them from being used with each other.

author: pierl2000

body:
Thanks for the report @pierl2000. This looks like a vendor specific issue related to Qualcomm [PAL](https://docs.qualcomm.com/doc/80-70014-16/topic/pal_sw.html)  (`Pal Stream Open Error (ffffffea)`). Also see [this](https://git.codelinaro.org/clo/le/platform/hardware/qcom/audio/-/blob/LE.UM.7.4.1.c4-00500-sa525m-tele/hal-pal/AudioStream.cpp?ref_type=tags).

Can you test [these](https://github.com/Cabbache/scrcpy/compare/forward-client-microphone...Cabbache:scrcpy:qualcomm-pal-test?expand=1) changes? https://github.com/Cabbache/scrcpy/tree/qualcomm-pal-test

Just for information, does this fix the issue:

```diff
diff --git app/src/compat.h app/src/compat.h
index 296d1a9ff..59a2771a2 100644
--- app/src/compat.h
+++ app/src/compat.h
@@ -103,7 +103,7 @@ long nrand48(unsigned short xsubi[3]);
 long jrand48(unsigned short xsubi[3]);
 #endif
 
-#ifndef HAVE_REALLOCARRAY
+#if !defined(HAVE_REALLOCARRAY) || (defined(__ANDROID_API__) && __ANDROID_API__ < 29)

author: rom1v

body:
> Just for information, does this fix the issue:
> 
> ```diff
> diff --git app/src/compat.h app/src/compat.h
> index 296d1a9ff..59a2771a2 100644
> --- app/src/compat.h
> +++ app/src/compat.h
> @@ -103,7 +103,7 @@ long nrand48(unsigned short xsubi[3]);
>  long jrand48(unsigned short xsubi[3]);
>  #endif
>  
> -#ifndef HAVE_REALLOCARRAY
> +#if !defined(HAVE_REALLOCARRAY) || (defined(__ANDROID_API__) && __ANDROID_API__ < 29)
>  void *reallocarray(void *ptr, size_t nmemb, size_t size);
>  #endif
>  
> ```
> 
> EDIT: fixed the condition ?

This declares `reallocarray` when `__ANDROID_API__ < 29` (and links to `/system/lib64/libc.so`)
which may not be desired, if truly targetting `__ANDROID_API__ < 29` (older `/system/lib64/libc.so` doesn't contain `reallocarray`)

if you want to inline the condition, then:
when `__ANDROID_API__ < 29`: it should use fallback `reallocarray` defined in `compat.c` (declare and define instead of just declare)

```diff
diff --git a/app/src/compat.c b/app/src/compat.c
index 785f843..289f54f 100644
--- a/app/src/compat.c
+++ b/app/src/compat.c
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@
 #include "config.h"
 
 #include <assert.h>
-#ifndef HAVE_REALLOCARRAY
+#if !defined(HAVE_REALLOCARRAY) || (defined(__ANDROID_API__) && __ANDROID_API__ < 29)
 # include <errno.h>
 #endif
 #include <stdlib.h>
@@ -98,7 +98,7 @@ long jrand48(unsigned short xsubi[3]) {
 #endif
 #endif
 
-#ifndef HAVE_REALLOCARRAY
+#if !defined(HAVE_REALLOCARRAY) || (defined(__ANDROID_API__) && __ANDROID_API__ < 29)
 void *reallocarray(void *ptr, size_t nmemb, size_t size) {
     size_t bytes;
     if (__builtin_mul_overflow(nmemb, size, &bytes)) {
```

The difference here is that I didn't touch audio handling; I'm just letting it continue with stock audio support (A11+ only) instead of trying to hack in root-enabled audio on older devices. ~~And I believe the package used in that was `com.android.systemui` instead of the metapackage for the core system (`android`).~~ Edit: the PR you mentioned actually did use `android` as the new package context like I did here. Edit 2: I reverted `FakeContext.java` **completely** due to it not affecting the clipboard's sync abilities no matter what I did. I no longer touch this like the other PR did.

body: I'm setting this as a draft until we can figure out what we need to do in order to get bidirectional clipboard functionality working. Also, I'm considering adding in a check in the client implementation to detect whether `--root` would even be needed for a secure display, and acting as if it was omitted when the device doesn't prevent `AID_SHELL` from actually making a secure display; this way, the flag would only actually change how the program operates where necessary for the desired outcome.
author: Giantvince1

body: Okay, I've got it figured out for real this time; clipboard is truly bidirectional by bypassing the usual Java package checks we obey and pass while `shell` but fail if we try as `system`. Instead it now just creates its own **direct** Binder IPC call in raw form, reusing the exact format that the existing interface provided so that no other code had to be modified for the new clipboard handling while `--root` is passed. I also tested everything else again just to be sure it all works; even audio support is completely intact.

body: Sorry about that; I'm still trying to figure out getting the clipboard to work. Even using the raw Binder interface via Parcel is returning empty data on reads, and it also refuses to let me instantiate a listener, which made me resort to polling. The only thing I can think of doing to get clipboard access working is to make it a stub sub-helper that talks to the main Java process; otherwise device to PC clipboard sync will just be broken when it runs as UID 1000.
author: Giantvince1

body: After endless trial and error, I cannot get the clipboard to work bidirectionally. The only option we have is to spawn two processes in this path: one that's spawned without an `su` call so it gains the context of `com.android.shell` which has clipboard read/write access unrestricted, and the main server process that handles the rest. The two processes would use some form of IPC internally so that the existing server code can receive and send clipboard data to the helper inside `com.android.shell` context. If we don't do that, we'll be completely stuck unable to read the clipboard or register the callback listener when using root access to create a secure display. The good part, however, is that refactoring the clipboard manager to support both the existing path and the potential new path is feasible; it would just involve some form of IPC between the existing clipboard manager and the helper stub so it can communicate back to the client side transparently, and that the IPC path is only used when the JAR is ran as UID 1000.

body:
> This PR introduces a new flag `--otg-mirror` which enables OTG-like HID input (via UHID) while maintaining the *video stream* (via ADB) in a single session.

What's the difference with:

```bash
scrcpy --keyboard=uhid --mouse=uhid
# or
scrcpy -KM
```

?
author: rom1v

body:
I appreciate the feedback!

To be completely honest, I didn't verify if `-KM` existed before starting this deep dive. I spent a lot of time analyzing the codebase to build what I thought was missing, so realizing `-KM` exists is definitely a facepalm moment for me! 😅

However, I think my 'reinvention of the wheel' actually produced a slightly different wheel that might be valuable:

While `scrcpy -KM` is a "Mixed Mode" (HID Input + ADB Control), my implementation of `--otg-mirror` acts as a **Strict Mode**. It explicitly sets `disable_adb_control = true`, which completely cuts off the ADB control channel.

This guarantees that **zero** ADB input events (like Volume keys, Home, Back, or clipboard sync) can be injected, relying 100% on the HID path. For specific use cases (like competitive gaming or avoidance of anti-cheat heuristics that flag ADB injections), this strict separation offers a cleaner 'physical device' emulation than `-KM`.

I'm happy to close this if you feel `-KM` is sufficient, but I do think this 'Strict HID' mode has unique merit.

**Extra Note:**  
> I used ChatGPT to refine the wording here ..because I don’t know English well and I wanted the explanation to be written clearly.

body:
## Changes Summary This PR implements audio isolation for apps running on virtual displays via the `--new-display` flag. It introduces AppMonitor functionality to detect foreground apps and restrict audio capture to their UIDs, along with two new CLI options for compatibility. However, there is a critical integrity issue with the screen.c file. **Type:** feature|mixed **Components Affected:** Audio subsystem (AudioPlaybackCapture, AudioDirectCapture), Virtual display capture (NewDisplayCapture, SurfaceCapture), CLI argument parsing (cli.c, options.h), Server-client communication (server.c, Server.java), System service wrappers (ActivityManager, PackageManager, ServiceManager), Event injection (Controller.java)
Files Changed | File | Summary | Change | Impact | |:-----|:--------|:------:|:------:| | `app/src/cli.c` | Added two new CLI options: --audio-ignore-apps and --audio-filter-apps for controlling app-based audio isolation | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `app/src/options.h` | Extended struct scrcpy_options with audio_ignore_apps and audio_filter_apps fields | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/src/options.c` | Initialized new audio options to NULL in default options | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/src/server.c` | Added parameter forwarding for audio_ignore_apps and audio_filter_apps to server invocation | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/src/scrcpy.c` | Propagates audio_ignore_apps and audio_filter_apps from CLI options to server parameters | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `app/src/screen.c` | CRITICAL ISSUE: File completely replaced with Java code (Controller.java content) instead of C code | ✏️ | 🔴 | | `app/src/server.h` | Added audio_ignore_apps and audio_filter_apps to server_params structure | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `...rc/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/Options.java` | Added audioIgnoreApps and audioFilterApps list fields with getter methods and parsing logic | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `...src/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/Server.java` | Integrated AudioPlaybackCapture with NewDisplayCapture for audio isolation; added logging for audio source configuration | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `...nymobile/scrcpy/audio/AudioPlaybackCapture.java` | Added setTargetUid() method and UID-based audio mixing rule logic using reflection to create AudioPolicy with RULE_MATCH_UID | ✏️ | 🔴 | | `...a/com/genymobile/scrcpy/control/Controller.java` | Enhanced key character injection to handle unmapped characters by creating KeyEvent directly | ✏️ | 🟢 | | `.../genymobile/scrcpy/video/NewDisplayCapture.java` | Implemented AppMonitor thread to detect foreground apps on virtual display and dynamically update AudioPlaybackCapture UID; added audioIgnoreApps/audioFilterApps filtering | ✏️ | 🔴 | | `...genymobile/scrcpy/wrappers/ActivityManager.java` | Added getRunningTasks(int maxNum) method to retrieve foreground app information; minor code cleanup | ✏️ | 🟡 | | `.../genymobile/scrcpy/wrappers/PackageManager.java` | NEW: Created PackageManager wrapper using reflection to access IPackageManager service for app UID/info lookups | ➕ | 🟡 | | `.../genymobile/scrcpy/wrappers/ServiceManager.java` | Added PackageManager singleton instance and getter method; minor comment cleanup | ✏️ | 🟢 |
Architecture Impact - **New Patterns:** App monitoring background thread pattern, UID-based audio routing via reflection-based AudioPolicy, Foreground app detection on virtual displays - **Dependencies:** Added: PackageManager wrapper for reflection-based service access - **Coupling:** NewDisplayCapture now tightly coupled to AudioPlaybackCapture for dynamic UID updates. Server.java now must instantiate and configure AudioPlaybackCapture before passing to NewDisplayCapture. - **Breaking Changes:** CRITICAL: app/src/screen.c file has been replaced with Java code - this is a file integrity corruption
**Risk Areas:** CRITICAL: screen.c file corruption - contains Java code instead of C code, will cause build failure, Reflection-heavy implementation in AudioPlaybackCapture and ActivityManager - relies on private Android APIs that may change across versions, Background AppMonitor thread creation without documented lifecycle guarantees - could lead to race conditions if not carefully managed, Audio UID switching logic may cause audio interruptions during app transitions, PackageManager wrapper uses reflection to access IPackageManager - potential compatibility issues across Android versions, AppMonitor thread does not have exception handling around System.currentTimeMillis() or Thread.sleep() edge cases, Interaction between audioIgnoreApps and audioFilterApps filtering logic could be unclear to users
Suggestions - CRITICAL: Fix screen.c file - restore original C code from upstream/dev, do NOT merge until this is resolved - Add comprehensive documentation for --audio-ignore-apps and --audio-filter-apps interaction and use cases - Add try-catch around AppMonitor thread operations to handle exceptions gracefully - Document AppMonitor lifecycle and thread safety guarantees - Consider adding version-specific reflection helpers to handle Android API differences in AudioPlaybackCapture - Add unit tests for AppMonitor app detection logic with mocked ActivityManager - Verify that audio restart triggered by setTargetUid() doesn't cause noticeable artifacts - Add logging around UID changes in AudioPlaybackCapture for debugging - Consider versioning the reflection-based API calls to handle multiple Android API levels
Full review in progress... | Powered by diffray

body:
did NOT work because:
    * .desktop files do not interpret shell-style escaping like \$
    * $SHELL expands incorrectly or becomes empty
    * /bin/sh -c ends up with no valid command  
- Working Exec lines:
    Exec=/bin/bash -ic "scrcpy --pause-on-exit=if-error"
    Exec=/bin/bash -ic "scrcpy"
    because:
    * bash -i loads .bashrc properly
    * .desktop can parse this line correctly
    * No confusing escaping is needed

body:
> Now that the `SCRCPY_ICON_PATH` env. var. is not used anymore; is there still a way to customize the main app icon when running a new instance?

You can no longer cutomize a single icon. Instead, use `SCRCPY_ICON_DIR` to customize all the icons at once by passing a directory. If you want to change only one icon, copy all the others in that custom directory.

Looks like I also need #6664 to test devices with spaces in serial?

Everything works except `LOGD` can't print non-ASCII characters:

```text
 ./scrcpy -Vverbose --no-audio --no-control
scrcpy 3.3.4 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: ADB device found:
INFO:     -->   (usb)  adb-bd9d00cc-irloe7 (2)._adb-tls-connect._tcp            device  23117RK66C
DEBUG: Device serial: adb-bd9d00cc-irloe7 (2)._adb-tls-connect._tcp
DEBUG: Using server (portable): C:\Users\simon\Downloads\test testµ╡ïΦ»ò\scrcpy-server
C:\Users\simon\Downloads\test test测试\scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 44.9 MB/s (93312 bytes in 0.002s)       
[server] INFO: Device: [Xiaomi] Redmi 23117RK66C (Android 16)
```

(Notice scrcpy prints `Downloads\test testµ╡ïΦ»ò\` and ADB prints `Downloads\test test测试\`)

Googled and this fixes the issue:

```patch
diff --git a/app/src/util/log.c b/app/src/util/log.c
index c583b8fb..470f1e67 100644
--- a/app/src/util/log.c
+++ b/app/src/util/log.c
@@ -151,6 +151,10 @@ sc_sdl_log_print(void *userdata, int category, SDL_LogPriority priority,
 
 void
 sc_log_configure(void) {
+#ifdef _WIN32
+    SetConsoleOutputCP(CP_UTF8);
+#endif
+
     SDL_SetLogOutputFunction(sc_sdl_log_print, NULL);
     // Redirect FFmpeg logs to SDL logs
     av_log_set_callback(sc_av_log_callback);

```

That's a fair response. 

Scrcpy already ships with a few scripts by default, I just thought I'd share a somewhat user-friendly one, since a lot of users may not be devs. 

The reason I submitted this: There is a significant difficulty for most users when first learning how to use Scrcpy. 

This script alleviates that to some extent. I wanted to provide a low friction path: run the script, follow the instructions, and connect to Android phone/device.

The message is for an assertion:
 - `pointers.size() >= MAX_POINTERS` is checked just above
 - if `pointers.size() < maxFingers`, then a local id should be available (but it is not, something is very wrong, this is a programming error/assertion)

This is not just a runtime error signaling to the user that all ids are in used, so the current error message is more appropriate.

body:
I made the following changes:
 - enable VP8 and VP9 decoders in FFmpeg (in your commit directly)
 - update `develop.md` to mention VP8 and VP9 (in your commit directly)
 - fix VP8 recording (contrary to the other codecs, it does not produce config packets)
 - reject recording VP8 in MP4 (it's not supported)
 
Please test/review the [`vp9`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/commits/vp9/) branch.
author: rom1v

body:
Thanks for the updates. I tested the `vp9` branch on Onyx Boox Note X (Android 11), and found two issues.

Available encoders:

```text
--video-codec=vp8 --video-encoder=c2.android.vp8.encoder          (sw)
--video-codec=vp8 --video-encoder=OMX.google.vp8.encoder          (sw) (alias for c2.android.vp8.encoder)
--video-codec=vp9 --video-encoder=c2.android.vp9.encoder          (sw)
--video-codec=vp9 --video-encoder=OMX.google.vp9.encoder          (sw) (alias for c2.android.vp9.encoder)
```

VP8 works and is usable:

```bash
./run build-auto --video-codec=vp8 --max-size=1600 --video-bit-rate=32M --max-fps=5
```

```text
INFO: Texture: 1600x1200
```

VP8 recording to MKV also works:

```bash
./run build-auto --video-codec=vp8 --record=file-vp8.mkv
```

## VP9 is constrained to a very low resolution

VP9 streaming starts, but on the `vp9` branch it is constrained to a very low resolution:

```bash
./run build-auto --video-codec=vp9 --max-size=1600 --video-bit-rate=32M --max-fps=5
```

```text
INFO: Texture: 596x448
```

This makes the stream very blurry.

I added temporary debug logs around `VideoCapabilities`. The runtime reports:

```text
[server] DEBUG: Using video encoder: 'c2.android.vp9.encoder'
[server] DEBUG: Video codec supported widths: [2, 2048]
[server] DEBUG: Video codec supported heights: [2, 2048]
[server] DEBUG: Video codec max landscape size: 2048x448
[server] DEBUG: Video codec max portrait size: 448x2048
```

For comparison, my original branch without the new encoder maximum size constraint streams VP9 at `1600x1200` with the same device, encoder and options:

```bash
./run build-auto-oldcheck --video-codec=vp9 --max-size=1600 --video-bit-rate=32M --max-fps=5
```

```text
INFO: Texture: 1600x1200
```

So this looks like a regression related to the new encoder size constraint logic from #6766. For this VP9 encoder, it computes a max landscape size of `2048x448`, which is very restrictive for this 4:3 display.

## VP9 recording to MKV fails

VP9 recording to MKV fails:

```bash
./run build-auto --video-codec=vp9 --record=file.mkv
```

```text
ERROR: The first video packet is not a config packet
ERROR: Recording failed to file.mkv
ERROR: Recorder error
```

This looks similar to the VP8 config-packet issue fixed in c0fe9d84. I tested this local change:

```c
recorder->video_expects_config_packet =
    ctx->codec_id != AV_CODEC_ID_VP8
    && ctx->codec_id != AV_CODEC_ID_VP9;
```

With this change, VP9 recording works.
author: pngocthach

body:
> So this looks like a regression related to the new encoder size constraint logic from https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6766.

Some encoders (especially software encoders) report incorrect capabilities. #6849 adds an option `--ignore-video-encoder-constraints`.

> ERROR: The first video packet is not a config packet

Oh, that does not happen with my device (Pixel 8) when recording to VP9. But not expecting a config packet also works, so I will make that change.

Thank you.

body:
Oh, it's not necessarily a `Surface` issue (or at least not only): when the virtual display is resized, there is a fade-in/fade-out animation between the previous content (old size) and the new content (new size), so the old size is scaled and blended into the resized content for a few frames, causing a visual glitch.

It can be seen by recording and replaying in slow motion:

```
scrcpy --new-display=1024x768 --start-app=com.android.settings --flex-display --record=file.mp4
```

Then trigger one resize, then replay the file with VLC or mpv in slow motion (use `[` to slow down and `]` to speed up).

author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
> - `virtualDisplay.resize()` is called from the same thread as the encoding process (otherwise Android would internally accumulate resize calls)

I finally abandoned this approach: `virtualDisplay.resize()` itself is an asynchronous request, and multiple requests can accumulate faster than the display can actually update, so it did not make sense.

In the end, I implemented an event debouncer (based on timing, something I initially wanted to avoid) that applies a resize (by calling `virtualDisplay.resize()` only after a fixed delay (hardcoded to 300 ms), collapsing all resize events within that window into a single update.

In case you're wondering, the debouncer is implemented on the server side (not on the client side) for several reasons:
 - if the display size/rotation changes independently, any the pending client resizes must be canceled (this is not possible if the client resize events are sent after a delay)
 - what matters is the interval between actual `resize()` calls on the server side (if the client sends several resizes over wifi and the network lags, those requests could otherwise be applied in rapid succession once they arrive) _(this is a minor concern in practice)_
 - in the future, we may be able to avoid a time-based debouncer and instead trigger resizes precisely when the display is "ready" (whatever that means), which would inherently be a server-side concern

Aha! `--min-size-alignment=2` is actually sufficient to avoid the crash. Do you have an explanation why it is needed?
author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
> Aha! `--min-size-alignment=2` is actually sufficient to avoid the crash.

:tada:

> Do you have an explanation why it is needed?

Previously, scrcpy always used dimensions that were multiples of 8. Since #6766, it uses the alignment requirements provided by the codec video capabilities. It seems that the software encoder in the emulator does not correctly declares its alignment requirements, so when trying to encode at an unsupported resolution, it fails.

[EDIT] Not sure if it's again a problem coming from the emulator. The thing is, when I rotate, the window size changes but the content only temporarily rotates, then it comes back to the default orientation ~~(and it gets stretched to fit the new window size). Weird behavior…~~ Haha I'm stupid, I think it's just the normal behavior you see when you rotate your phone. OK until here. However: And the bug I see is in fact the window that does not change its orientation, but the virtual  display does think so, thus the content gets cropped to a square.

[EDIT bis] I can reproduce the issue even if I set `DEBOUNCE_DELAY_MS = 0`.

[EDIT ter] The bug occurs only when going from landscape to portrait orientation.

[EDIT quater] Well, I don't know what should happen. Without `--flex-display` the window is fixed and the content rotates one quarter turn (but mouse actions fail since their position doesn't match the new orientation of the content). Without `--new-display` the window is fixed but the content too, i.e. it can't be rotated (with <kbd>MOD</kbd>+<kbd>r</kbd>).

[EDIT quinquies] I tested on current `master` branch: Without `--new-display`, MOD+left/right arrow works (the content turns by quarters with the window) but MOD+r doesn't work (requires landscape mode but remains fixed); with `--new-display`, MOD+left/right arrow still works and MOD+r works correctly too (window resized and content stretched to fit it while remaining in the same orientation). On `flex-display.5` branch: Without `--new-display`, it behaves the same as `master`; with `--new-display`, arrows work the same way but MOD+r behaves differently since the content rotates inside a fixed window as explained under "EDIT quater"; and with `--flex-display`, arrows still work and MOD+r also works except for the issue I reported, meaning, content can be cropped to a square when going back from landscape to portrait mode because the window isn't resized as expected.

body:
> I suspect it is one of the "capture glitches". If you record (with `--record=file.mp4`), is the glitch recorded?

I'm not sure. I tested to go in landscape mode then only decrease both dimensions. I tried to playback at slow speed and although I can see fading effects I don't see the virtual display becoming bigger. The issue is the window itself (the container, not the content) that jumps to a bigger size even though it should only ever shrink. It's like if there were additional black borders on the right and bottom momentarily (the virtual display doesn't seem to grow with the window, or maybe just one frame, but hard to tell from the recording). My logs above are repeatable in the sense that after `DisplayMonitor: onDisplayConfigurationChanged` there is a `resize_display` with a bigger size (not due to me) before another `resize_display` that resets the expected size. Notice that there is no `can_resize` log between the wrong `resize_display` and the last good one. This issue doesn't always happen, but very often (in landscape mode). Tell me if you'd like me to share videos.
author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
@anotheruserofgithub

> I've observed another issue, the VD is not always resized on rotation change if I rotate too "quickly" [source](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4320424217)

> I think I see why this can happen (but cannot reproduce). [source](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4320424217)

The problem is that when the app is rotated:
 1. the frame dimensions change,
 2. so the window is resized on the client side,
 3. so a "resize" event is received,
 4. so a "display resize" request is sent to the server.

The loop is interrupted if the size does not change on the server. However, if you rotate quickly, it can change during the round trip.

In that case, the server considers it a "client resize" (the client emitted the "display resize" request), so in return is sets the `client_resized` flag in the protocol, As a result, the client does not resize, which is incorrect.

This is fixed by the commit `Ignore frame-triggered resize events`, which prevents the client from sending a "display resize" request when the resize event is triggered by its own resize call due to a frame-size change (in other words, it breaks the link between 3 and 4 in the list above).
author: rom1v

body:
I investigated a bit more by logging [`reason`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/1762fff20dca0dfff0333c2046b4ca2cefbcec3b/server/src/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/video/NewDisplayCapture.java#L244) and [`displayRotation`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/1762fff20dca0dfff0333c2046b4ca2cefbcec3b/server/src/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/video/NewDisplayCapture.java#L328). It turns out, the issue is that after MOD+r the client resizes the window which actually sends a resize request to the server since it is a flex display. ~~Due to the debounce delay,~~ this messes up the tracker, which at some point thinks the new size was caused by the client (`reason = 8`) instead of internally (`reason = 2`).

[EDIT] Haha we got there at the same time. ;) I'll look at your fix right now.

body:
On `flex-display.7`:
1. First bullet point of https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4320667302 is still present.
2. Second bullet point of https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4320667302 is fixed.
3. Issue of https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4321652487 is still present.
4. I tried [fullscreen mode](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/b02950838a4351a740583839bd6cd28e90057a6f/app/src/screen.c#L791-L794) with MOD+f ([rotation=0], portrait), since my emulator's codec limits are lower than my screen size, the virtual display is resized (with black borders) to respect the aspect ratio (thus it appears as if it were in landscape). On MOD+r ([rotation=1]), it temporarily rotates then gets resized to my screen's aspect ratio again (still in landscape). That seems to be consistent. Then on MOD+f it goes back in windowed mode ([rotation=1]) but the window is in portrait size. I think this size is stored when going in fullscreen so that you can reset it when going back in windowed mode. However, I would have expected the virtual display to appear in landscape size at this point. Is it possible? _This isn't specific to flex display_ or emulator limitations, I think there is an inconsistency in the sense that if I never manually resize the display, [rotation=0] should always appear in portrait mode and [rotation=1] in landscape mode, however on exiting fullscreen the display is in [rotation=1] with portrait (instead of landscape) size. Apparently it would just be a matter of checking the rotation when switching to windowed mode and exchange width/height if it differs from the rotation that was active when going to fullscreen mode. Or maybe what I'm saying just doesn't make sense?
author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
For 1 and 3, could you please make a video capture? It's not clear to me.

For 4, I explicitly disable the "pending resize" mechanism when leaving fullscreen for flex displays, as I expect the window to be restored in its pre-fullscreen size (contrary to what happens for non-flex displays). But it's quite arbitrary.

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/348a8f8bf87e4d299f10c1601d98fd024861f593/app/src/screen.c#L827

body:
Here are videos. Please let me know if you need more information.

- Issue 1:

  [issue1.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e9c65235-7b64-40bc-98e0-1c661b6c8268)

- Issue 3:

  [issue3.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/859c5fe9-f2fe-4f21-885f-0474816e742e)

- Issue 4:

  [issue4.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dda3fc6c-f7bd-4b14-bdac-207caf9e91eb)

  > For 4, I explicitly disable the "pending resize" mechanism when leaving fullscreen for flex displays, as I expect the window to be restored in its pre-fullscreen size
  
  Yes the previous size should be restored. My concern is only about rotation. At the end of the video, I would have expected width & height to be swapped since I did a rotation with MOD+r during fullscreen. Unfortunately I can't really test without `--flex-display` because of issue 1. But anyway, I don't think rotation is a relevant feature in fullscreen (at least for flex display since I guess it should render the same before and after rotation), is it?
  
  [EDIT] I could test on `master` without `--flex-display` and it works as I would expect, i.e., it respects rotation when leaving fullscreen (and rotation obviously makes sense in this case). However, simply commenting out https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/b02950838a4351a740583839bd6cd28e90057a6f/app/src/screen.c#L791-L794 won't work in case the size has to be constrained inside codec limits like with my emulator. On leaving fullscreen mode, the orientation is indeed landscape, however the window size is too small. That is due to keeping aspect ratio inside constraints in fullscreen. The fix would be to somehow store not only the previous size but also the previous rotation, then when switching to windowed mode, check if rotation has changed and swap width & height in this case. Or maybe there's a simpler way to achieve this. But again, is rotation relevant for flex display in fullscreen? If not, then there's no need to try to fix it.

body:
Thank you very much :+1:

I fixed (1):
 - for some reason, any exception in the display/window listener was silently ignored, so I added 390ee5d4e94ffbbf91991e039d4defc6692f6bb4
 - in the `DisplayMonitor` callback, `tracker.onChanged(props);` triggered a `NullPointerException`, because `tracker == null` when `!flexDisplay`: https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/flex-display.7/server/src/main/java/com/genymobile/scrcpy/video/NewDisplayCapture.java#L235
 
 Fixed on `flex-display.8`.
 
 I'll check the others later.

body:
(3) was caused by size constraints which should not preserve aspect ratio for flex display. Fixed in `flex-display.9`

Maybe (4) is also related to size constraints. Please retest on `flex-display.9`. I assume that `800x600 [rotation=0]` gives the same result as `800x600 [rotation=1]` in apps (what matters for the orientation is whether width > height).
Btw, on the client side, scrcpy does not even know the display orientation.

On `flex-display.9`:

1. Fixed by https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4333306161.
   
2. Fixed by https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4328610484.
   
3. I agree that the aspect ratio should not be preserved, the size must only fit inside codec limits. Thanks for noticing and fixing. But this is not the issue I was trying to show. The issue is that the window often makes excursions beyond its bottom right (just one frame), i.e., it grows in size when it should not (again, only in landscape mode). In this video I show first in portrait then in landscape. Surely I'm not the only one having this issue, am I? Can't you reproduce? It seems to be the client doing extra calls to `resize_display()` with a larger size (where does it come from?) but I don't know what triggers them. Maybe there is some debug flag in the code that I can enable to print more logs?
   
   [issue3.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82f63658-81fc-43d7-9757-e89af0f95056)
   
5. I have to manually tweak codec limits to 2560 otherwise the emulator crashes in fullscreen on my 4k monitor (probably, reported capabilities are wrong). So you will see that the virtual display can't fit the full width of my screen. This won't change the demonstration. My previous assumption (and yours) "that `800x600 [rotation=0]` gives the same result as `800x600 [rotation=1]` in apps" is wrong, at least for Settings, because it reserves space for the navigation bar (by the way, can this be removed somehow since it is blank on VD?). On portrait mode it is located at the bottom but on landscape at the right. Well, normally. As you can see on the video, this is correct at the beginning before entering fullscreen, then while being in fullscreen I do a rotation (still OK), and on leaving fullscreen you reset the previous portrait size, however the app thinks it remained in landscape (which is true because `[rotation=1]`), hence the navigation bar is in the reverse location at the end. So "my dream" would be to be able to automatically swap width & height in this case when leaving fullscreen such that the window is shown with landscape orientation, but I don't know if it's really worth the extra work. What are your thoughts on that?

body:
Thank you for all your tests.

About https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4337803046, I think (3) is not a scrcpy issue (maybe SDL/Gnome/Wayland/X11…). By the way, I cannot reproduce on Gnome either. 

About (4):

> So "my dream" would be to be able to automatically swap width & height in this case when leaving fullscreen such that the window is shown with landscape orientation, but I don't know if it's really worth the extra work. What are your thoughts on that?

For non-flex displays, if the orientation changes (or any size change) while in fullscreen, the size change is applied when leaving the fullscreen mode. For flex displays, this cannot work, since the size is reset to adapt to the fullscreen mode whenever it changes. Rotation is meaningless in this context (whether `rotation=0` or `rotation=1` internally, what matters is the size (except issue 4 in https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4337803046)). So I think I will keep it like that. Btw, the rotation state is not known on the client side (only the size is known).

So I think the current version is ready to merge (I will wait a bit more to catch more bugs before merging).

Also if I set `--new-display` without specifying the size, it is forced to 1600x640 but only at startup, not on manual resizing:
```
VERBOSE: input: start app "com.android.settings"
VERBOSE: Track resize: 1280x512
ERROR: Could not set window position: wayland cannot position non-popup windows
[server] INFO: Starting app "Settings" [com.android.settings] on display 5...
VERBOSE: resize_display(1600, 640)
```
For the device that's the portrait orientation. Then if I rotate to landscape I have the same issue again, but this time it's the height that is forced to ...x960, also in a few steps if needed (e.g., -> 840 -> 960). All of this is automatic upon MOD+r:
```
VERBOSE: input: rotate device

That was with Wayland. Then I tried with X11: `SDL_VIDEODRIVER=x11 ./scrcpy [...]` and I got the usual issue as I showed in my earlier videos, not what I described here (i.e., the window sometimes bounces for like one frame but it doesn't attempt to grow as much as possible).

Haha I knew rotation with flex display would be so much fun. ^^

body:
@anotheruserofgithub https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4344753479

> I tested your pre-built binary on Debian 13

Which version?

Because `Never increase dimensions when applying alignment` (fb1db3a476899d4661da9f267ae13608506777f7) in `flex-display.10` is supposed to fix your issue:

```
[server] VERBOSE: NewDisplayCapture: requestResize(383, 915)

VERBOSE: resize_display(383, 910)
VERBOSE: input: resize display 383x910
```

> Does rotating the display even make sense in a flex display?

The problem is that it can happen even if we don't want to, if an app decides to rotate on its own, or if you press <kbd>MOD</kbd>+<kbd>r</kbd> (and it's convenient to be able to rotate with <kbd>MOD</kbd>+<kbd>r</kbd>).
author: rom1v

body:
> Does rotating the display even make sense in a flex display?

That's the point, in principle it hardly makes sense. I see two cases where it might be useful:
- If you want to keep the exact same size of the display but in reverse orientation, it would be difficult to achieve manually (at least without looking at the logs), while it is quick and exact with the MOD+r shortcut.
- As I said in https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4337803046 (point 4), the app may not present itself exactly the same in both orientations, e.g. because of the navigation bar. But it's fair to say that such small differences don't really matter in practice.

[EDIT] As @rom1v said, if an app decides to rotate on its own, that could be a problem.

author: rom1v

body:
> ERROR: Could not set window position: wayland cannot position non-popup windows

Yes that's a well-known issue with Wayland, Qt has the same problem, also see [this SDL doc](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-wayland#sdl_setwindowposition-doesnt-work-on-non-popup-windows). However, you should always be able to set the window size, and in fact it's quite important for flex displays. Only the position is impossible, and indeed with a debug build scrcpy crashed on the assert (I was about to report it). Is there a way to retrieve the current SDL video backend at runtime in order to disable the assert only for Wayland?

There is [this other issue](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-wayland#the-application-icon-cant-be-set-via-sdl_setwindowicon), I don't know if you could find a workaround e.g. by adding a .desktop file (really not important):

> DEBUG: Using icon from SCRCPY_ICON_DIR: app/data/scrcpy.png
> WARN: Could not set window icon: wayland: cannot set icon; required xdg_toplevel_icon_v1 protocol not supported

---

Yesterday evening I did other experiments by enabling more SDL logs:
```diff
diff --git a/app/src/scrcpy.c b/app/src/scrcpy.c
index 8001580b..78429c81 100644
--- a/app/src/scrcpy.c
+++ b/app/src/scrcpy.c
@@ -368,6 +368,8 @@ init_sdl_gamepads(void) {
 
 enum scrcpy_exit_code
 scrcpy(struct scrcpy_options *options) {
+    SDL_SetLogPriorities(SDL_LOG_PRIORITY_VERBOSE);
+    SDL_SetHint(SDL_HINT_EVENT_LOGGING, "1");
     static struct scrcpy scrcpy;
 #ifndef NDEBUG
     // Detect missing initializations
```

On Debian, the video backend was indeed Wayland.
On Ubuntu however, it falled back to X11 all along:
```
INFO: This compositor lacks support for the fifo-v1 protocol; falling back to XWayland for GPU performance reasons (set SDL_VIDEO_DRIVER=wayland to override)
INFO: Detected XWayland
DEBUG: SDL chose video backend 'x11'
```
If I prepend the command with `SDL_VIDEO_DRIVER=wayland` (or `export` the symbol), I can force it to use Wayland:
```
DEBUG: SDL chose video backend 'wayland'
```

Therefore, issue 3 from https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4337803046 **happens on X11, not on Wayland**.
Whether it's X11 itself or the SDL backend for X11 that has a problem, I don't know, I should find a way to print X11 logs somehow (do you have a clue?).

It would be interesting to know if you can reproduce the issue on X11 with flex display in landscape orientation: `SDL_VIDEO_DRIVER=x11 scrcpy --new-display --flex-display [opts]` then MOD+r to `[rotation=1]` and resize.

I wanted to test on Windows too but for some reason I couldn't run the same emulator for long enough.

---

This explains the issue with bigger window size in https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4344753479.

The thing is that I set a display scale factor (on Ubuntu 200%, on Debian 125%) and this is not handled well somewhere in scrcpy. If I reset to 100% there is no issue. At 200% even the mouse pointer is shown twice bigger.

With X11 there is no such issue because it doesn't handle this scale factor at all, it works as if everything were 100%.

As you can read in [this SDL doc](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-wayland#importing-external-surfaces-into-sdl-windows), there are two kinds of size: the logical size ("window size") and the backbuffer size ("window size in pixels"). It's already used in scrcpy for handling HiDPI coordinates.

Basically you can see these logs where one size is twice bigger than the other:
```
INFO: SDL EVENT: SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED (timestamp=366748241 windowid=4 event=SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED data1=456 data2=945)
INFO: SDL EVENT: SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_PIXEL_SIZE_CHANGED (timestamp=366766436 windowid=4 event=SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_PIXEL_SIZE_CHANGED data1=912 data2=1890)
```

I'm not sure but I guess the virtual display should set its size with the logical size, hence I tried replacing `SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_PIXEL_SIZE_CHANGED` by `SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED` in `sc_screen_handle_event()` so that `sc_screen_on_resize()` sends the logical size, but it wasn't enough to fix the issue. Well, the reported issue that in landscape orientation the window was forced to grow more than required, _this one is fixed_. But now I see that the virtual display only fills the top-left quarter of the scrcpy window, or in other words _the window is twice bigger than the display (and the mouse twice bigger than usual too)_, so there is still an inconsistency somewhere. See also [this SDL doc](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-wayland#legacy-dpi-unaware-applications-are-blurry), it might explain the problem.

Below I copied the logs printed when I just launch scrcpy and quit as soon as the virtual display is shown.
Notice that you can print even more detailed logs if you set `WAYLAND_DEBUG=1`.

My screen has a logical size (at scale 200%) of 1920x1080, I suspect this is the reason why the window height goes from the requested 1230 down to 944.

<details>
<summary>Logs Wayland 200%</summary>

```
$ SDL_VIDEO_DRIVER=wayland ./run x -Vverbose --no-audio --flex-display --new-display=570x1230/220 --min-size-alignment=2 --start-app=com.android.settings
scrcpy 3.3.4 <https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy>
INFO: App name: SDL Application
INFO: App version: <unspecified>

INFO: SDL revision: SDL-3.4.4-release-3.4.4-1-gb14027228

DEBUG: Device serial: 127.0.0.1:6555

x/server/scrcpy-server: 1 file pushed, 0 skipped. 424.1 MB/s (1080687 bytes in 0.002s)
INFO: SDL EVENT: SDL_EVENT_MOUSE_ADDED (timestamp=35883177 which=1)
INFO: SDL EVENT: SDL_EVENT_KEYBOARD_ADDED (timestamp=35906523 which=2)
[server] INFO: Device: [Genymobile] Genymotion Phone (Android 15)

DEBUG: Server connected

body:
@rom1v From https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4358805020:

> I consider the resize glitch (which apparently occurs in some cases with a non-100% display scale factor on Wayland) non-blocking for this PR. It's not yet clear whether the issue comes from Wayland, Gnome, SDL or scrcpy itself. It can be addressed later once we have a proper solution.

Just to be clear, there are two issues related to flex display for now:

- The resize glitch in landscape orientation (or at least, after manual rotation by MOD+r) concerns X11, not Wayland. Extract from https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4353169629:
  > Therefore, issue 3 from [#6772 (comment)](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4337803046) **happens on X11, not on Wayland**.

- The window size forced too big is due to fractional scaling on Wayland, that is not handled correctly in scrcpy (more info in my next comment). Extract from https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4353169629:
  > The thing is that I set a display scale factor (on Ubuntu 200%, on Debian 125%) and this is not handled well somewhere in scrcpy. If I reset to 100% there is no issue. At 200% even the mouse pointer is shown twice bigger.
author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
Extract from https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4353169629:

> As you can read in [this SDL doc](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-wayland#importing-external-surfaces-into-sdl-windows), there are two kinds of size: the logical size ("window size") and the backbuffer size ("window size in pixels"). It's already used in scrcpy for handling HiDPI coordinates.

See also `sc_screen_hidpi_scale_coords()`.

Just to be clear on the nomenclature I'll be using:
- physical size (or "backbuffer size", or "size in pixels") = the number of pixels in the screen/monitor,
- logical size = size after (fractional) scaling, e.g., a physical size of 1920x1080 with a scaling of 200% means a logical size of 960x540, hence everything appears twice bigger.

> I'm not sure but I guess the virtual display should set its size with the logical size, hence I tried replacing `SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_PIXEL_SIZE_CHANGED` by `SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED` in `sc_screen_handle_event()` so that `sc_screen_on_resize()` sends the logical size […] But now I see that the virtual display only fills the top-left quarter of the scrcpy window, or in other words _the window is twice bigger than the display (and the mouse twice bigger than usual too)_, so there is still an inconsistency somewhere. See also [this SDL doc](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-wayland#legacy-dpi-unaware-applications-are-blurry), it might explain the problem.

Followed by https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4353246673.

I don't claim that the virtual display should be in logical size, I don't know, I just tried with physical size and dividing the session packet data by 2 (my scaling is 200%) but I couldn't make it work:
```
[server] VERBOSE: NewDisplayCapture: constrained size = 500x466
[server] VERBOSE: DisplayMonitor: onDisplayConfigurationChanged(3)
[server] VERBOSE: DisplayMonitor: 500x360 [rotation=1] -> 500x466 [rotation=1]
[server] DEBUG: +++++ width = 250
[server] DEBUG: +++++ height = 233
WARN: Unexpected video size: 500x466 (expected 250x233)
WARN: The encoder did not respect the requested size, please retry with a lower resolution (-m/--max-size)
```

It looks to me like there is a bug in scrcpy about scaling, I mean, a wrong use of physical size in place of logical size somewhere with flex display.

The problem is as follows: `SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED` holds the logical size of the window, whereas `SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_PIXEL_SIZE_CHANGED` holds the _physical size_ of the window and it's the size that is passed to the virtual display in `sc_screen_on_resize()`. In return, the virtual display sends the constrained physical size in a session packet, then on a new frame the client retrieves the new size in `sc_screen_apply_frame()`, which forwards to `set_content_size()`, which arrives in `resize_for_content()`, which calls `sc_sdl_set_window_size()`, which is a wrapper around [`SDL_SetWindowSize()`](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/SDL_SetWindowSize). But as explained [here](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-wayland#importing-external-surfaces-into-sdl-windows), this function expects a **logical size**, not a physical size.

Indeed, `sc_screen_init()` sets the `SDL_WINDOW_HIGH_PIXEL_DENSITY` flag as explained [here](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/README-highdpi#solution): https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/3de3325b984ff5b83b22b94fd9c8adba901c7136/app/src/screen.c#L422

<details>
<summary>Here is an experiment where I simply rotate the virtual display, it goes from 500x400 to 1000x800 and so forth…</summary>

```
[…]
VERBOSE: input: rotate device

[server] VERBOSE: DisplayMonitor: onDisplayConfigurationChanged(3)

author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
@anotheruserofgithub 

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#discussion_r3173141319

> But a better approach would thus be to not allow to call `resize()` after `release()` even without explicit synchronization. If I'm right this can be readily achieved by calling `debouncer.join()`

I think it's fine to keep them synchronized and ignore the `resize()` call if `virtualDisplay == null`. In theory, if we removed the debouncer, the controller could request a `resize()` after `release()`, and it would be handled separately.

> However, `triggerResize()` should not even be called before the virtual display is created, otherwise that would mean discarding early resize requests (the requested size would be lost, right?).

The virtual display is created in `capture.start()`, which is called before sending the video dimensions to the client via `streamer.writeSessionMeta()`, which causes the window to open. So in practice, the window could not be resized before the virtual display is created. Therefore, there is no need for special handling of early resize requests, they can simply be ignored.


---

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4359684619

> I don't know if you'd like to solve this problem:
>
> > WARN: Could not set window icon: wayland: cannot set icon; required xdg_toplevel_icon_v1 protocol not supported

Not really important.

---

https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4360006349

> It looks to me like there is a bug in scrcpy about scaling

Will investigate. Thank you.
author: rom1v

body:
https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4363502234

> I think it's fine to keep them synchronized and ignore the `resize()` call if `virtualDisplay == null`. In theory, if we removed the debouncer, the controller could request a `resize()` after `release()`, and it would be handled separately.

That totally makes sense, so it's better to keep them synchronized, indeed. ;)

> The virtual display is created in `capture.start()`, which is called before sending the video dimensions to the client via `streamer.writeSessionMeta()`, which causes the window to open. So in practice, the window could not be resized before the virtual display is created. Therefore, there is no need for special handling of early resize requests, they can simply be ignored.

That's great! Thanks for the explanation. Therefore even `displayMonitor.start()` is safe at its current place. :)

---

What do you think about replacing the VD setter with a local synchronized block in `startNew()`, since you need one in `release()` anyway? (See https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#discussion_r3173141319, seventh bullet point, and proposition 1.)
author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
> What do you think about replacing the VD setter with a local synchronized block in startNew(), since you need one in release() anyway? (See https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#discussion_r3173141319, seventh bullet point, and proposition 1.)

It makes more sense to synchronized only the setter to me. But in practice that does not change a lot.

body:
After https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#discussion_r3176433980, it may be good to amend the description of [`--max-size`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/e4d578d74ac63a32fbbc9caf93fb283bc4e703c8/doc/video.md?plain=1#L16-L22) for flex display, saying that the aspect ratio is _not_ preserved in this case (there is already a special case for camera mirroring):
https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/e4d578d74ac63a32fbbc9caf93fb283bc4e703c8/doc/video.md?plain=1#L24-L28

And maybe it should be specified that the [`--crop`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/e4d578d74ac63a32fbbc9caf93fb283bc4e703c8/doc/video.md?plain=1#L191-L197) feature is unavailable with flex display (since it also mentions `--max-size`, which now is available with flex display but would be applied _before_ `--crop` if the latter was available):
https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/e4d578d74ac63a32fbbc9caf93fb283bc4e703c8/doc/video.md?plain=1#L204-L206
author: anotheruserofgithub

body:
For flex display:
- [`--window-width` and `--window-height`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/e4d578d74ac63a32fbbc9caf93fb283bc4e703c8/doc/window.md?plain=1#L22-L26) may not be great ~~because the VD would not fit the window initially.~~ [EDIT] It's more subtle than that. When setting an initial display size like `--new-display=400x300`, it takes this size and isn't resized to the window size, is it because of the resize tracker? When using `--new-display` without a size, it fits the window although I can't see the resize event in the logs (and the printed display/texture size is not that of the window), can you explain that?
- ~~[`--window-borderless`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/e4d578d74ac63a32fbbc9caf93fb283bc4e703c8/doc/window.md?plain=1#L37-L41) also seems useless because without borders the window would not be resizable (or does it depend on the windowing systems?).~~ [EDIT] @Tech-Tac replied (I don't see the message anymore) that "borderless windows can still be resized by the user in pretty much all windowing systems, often with a keyboard shortcut or a gesture". That's right, I wasn't aware of that. I just checked on Gnome: Super+Right-click or Alt+Space opens a menu where you can resize/move the window, or Alt+F8 to go directly in resize mode (be it with or without frame).

Of course those are not problematic, they work correctly even when used with a flex display. Maybe they deserve a warning, saying they are inappropriate? Or we just don't care?
author: anotheruserofgithub

body: [`--no-control`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/blob/e4d578d74ac63a32fbbc9caf93fb283bc4e703c8/doc/control.md?plain=1#L3-L11) must _not_ be allowed with `--flex-display` because on resizing this leads to a segmentation fault.

Everything is treated with physical size, i.e., as if I had 100% scaling.
Notice that width 512 is due to codec constraints while height 1808 is due to `--min-size-alignment`.
I see the window taking almost the entire height of my screen.

---

On Wayland:
```bash
SDL_VIDEO_DRIVER=wayland ./run x -Vverbose --no-audio --flex-display --new-display=570x1820/220 --max-size=2560 --min-size-alignment=16 --start-app=com.android.settings
```

[server] INFO: Starting app "Settings" [com.android.settings] on display 46...
INFO: SDL EVENT: SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_SHOWN (timestamp=318690461 windowid=4 event=SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_SHOWN data1=0 data2=0)
INFO: SDL EVENT: SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_EXPOSED (timestamp=318709304 windowid=4 event=SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_EXPOSED data1=0 data2=0)
VERBOSE: resize_display(556, 1968)
[…]
```
The new requested size 556x1968 is a downscaling of 1024x3616, the latter is 200% * 512x1808, it is constrained by the windowing system to fit entirely within the height of my 4k screen.
The "blow-up" of the window ceases as soon as the new texture size fits inside the current window physical size.
I see the window taking almost the entire height of my screen.

---

So, if we want the same behavior on X11 and Wayland when there is desktop scaling, I think the virtual display should be passed the physical size (as is the case now), but the programmatic window resizing should be in logical size.

body:
I've seen a fatal crash _without_ `--flex-display` with Wayland backend (not reproducible with X11 backend), independent of the desktop scaling:

When resizing the window toward a zero-sized rect, scrcpy crashes. Technically the minimum possible window size is supposed to be 1x1. None of the dimensions reaches 0 in the logs. In my tests the aspect ratio is about 570:1230 (width:height), so it's in portrait size (not sure it matters, just in case you can't reproduce).

I could reproduce both on Debian 13.3 (prebuilt binary) and Ubuntu 24.04 (manually compiled SDL & scrcpy).
On Debian, I get [the following error log](https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/blob/c699512adcc139ec1a355ff97cb2e5dbab3c9ac2/src/video/wayland/SDL_waylandvideo.c#L1931):
```
ERROR: Wayland display connection closed by server (fatal)
```
On Ubuntu, my desktop session crashes completely, i.e., I'm logged out and have to log in again.

The issue is **due to locking the aspect ratio** (https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6761).
When adding `--no-window-aspect-ratio-lock` the crash disappears (minimum window size is 1x1).

Not sure if it's an SDL bug? I tried to assert a non-zero window size in `sc_screen_update_content_rect()`, no change.
I suppose SDL (or Wayland) computes the new window size (based on the mouse delta) taking into account the requested aspect ratio, and ends up with either a zero dimension or a division by zero.

My worry is that users can lose all their unsaved work if their computer crashes like my Ubuntu.

body:
https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4379132934

> Yes, you should use 200x500. There is no inconsistency, your sample correctly prints the logical size when resizing.

> I observe this behavior both for scrcpy and the sample.

Indeed, I just found out that it falled back to the X11 backend all along… Forgot to add `SDL_VIDEO_DRIVER=wayland`.

Now with Wayland backend I can reproduce the same crash with the sample program as with scrcpy.

It's an SDL bug, they end up with 0 width or height that [they pass to `wp_viewport_set_destination()`](https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/blob/c65c8093377cfa286854a3ff1bed9668b9d2a255/src/video/wayland/SDL_waylandwindow.c#L479), while this function requires strictly positive values.

There is no good workaround that I can think of because even `SDL_SetWindowMinimumSize(window, 1, 1)` won't work. The problem is that they are using this min size kind of in reverse, i.e., they limit the larger dimension and compute the smaller one taking into account the aspect ratio.

I will clean up the sample and open an issue in SDL repo. See here: https://github.com/libsdl-org/SDL/issues/15520

body:
https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#pullrequestreview-4238019023 is solving the reverse problem of my initial suspicion in https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4383698649, so forget the latter one.

What I initially thought was unexpected, was when I start `--new-display=WxH` with a height (might be width as well) higher than my screen and with a `--min-size-alignment=A` bigger than 1 (take 16 to make it more obvious). Since the initial display size cannot fit entirely in my screen, the window is automatically resized by the window manager (or maybe by SDL; doesn't matter) to the highest possible height (it may also take a bit of margin). This is seen as a window resized event in SDL, that the client forwards to the server in order to resize the virtual display accordingly. Thus, from the server viewpoint, this is a client resize, hence it won't make the window match the new display size in return. Because of the minimum alignment, the new display size may be slightly smaller than the window size, therefore black borders are visible. I was expecting that at startup, the window matches the virtual display size, hence my concern in https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4383698649 (there were also other occasions where I saw a strange behavior, because of the misplaced `size.rotate()` bug, but this one was the easiest to observe and reproduce).

However, I now understand that this is viewed as a client resize, even though it was not triggered by the user but rather by the system. So, in the end I think it is the expected behavior, right?

@anotheruserofgithub https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4391103913

Good catch. :+1:

> So, in the end I think it is the expected behavior, right?

In theory, we could instead send a flag in the `RESIZE_DISPLAY` message from the client to the server to indicate whether the resize was initiated by the user or automatically. But that adds too much complexity for this specific corner case, so I think I'll just consider this the expected behavior :D

It could also be worked around by aligning the "optimal size" to a multiple of 16, but that feels a bit hacky and ad hoc.

The fact that we open a virtual display at a given size and then resize it immediately isn't great. But for now, it's not a big deal.

body:
> to indicate whether the resize was initiated by the user or automatically
>
> How would you even be able to detect that?

With the `sc_screen_track_resize()` mechanism (the resize is considered initiated by the user if we didn't explicitly call `set_window_size()` with that size recently).

> (https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6772#issuecomment-4404528815) what do you think of extending `RESIZE_DISPLAY` […]

If we wanted to preserve the rotation, I think the correct way would be to track the display rotation along with the `client_resize` flag in the session header.

However:

> mod+f -> mod+r -> mod+f -> should swap width & height

I don't think this is always true. In your example, it was, but for example:
 - start in landscape, windowed
 - switch to fullscreen (still landscape)
 - rotate with <kbd>MOD</kbd>+<kbd>r</kbd> → the device internal rotation changes from 0 to 1
 - since the screen is fullscreen, the display gets resized to the fullscreen size (with a "landscape size" but keeping rotation=1)
 - switch to another app (still landscape)
 - exit fullscreen → you do not want to rotate your original window size to portrait

> In the docs, I don't know if you'd like to mention the special case of flex display for the following options

Not really. IMO, the doc does not need to be exhaustive about corner cases or special restrictions.

If someone executes `scrcpy --new -x --crop=1:2:3:4`, it will get the error:

```
ERROR: --crop is not compatible with -x/--flex-display
```

which is sufficient.

author: rom1v

body:
Or maybe this option should only apply to max/min size, not alignment?

I guess the alignment reported by video capabilities will always work (except e.g. for emulators).

But I'm even wondering if the supported size is really wrong. In https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6769#issuecomment-4501772790 as well as in other issues you're assuming that the encoder reports incorrect capabilities. It seems more probable that the way you compute the maximum possible size is not appropriate, as additional logs in https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/pull/6769#issuecomment-4501157604 tend to suggest (IMHO).

What I mean is that I wouldn't be surprised if the encoder could handle only a limited number of pixels in total or a limited aspect ratio, something like that. Thus if you first retrieve the maximum allowed width and the encoder returns 2048, then if you ask it the maximum possible height it can handle for this width it will return 448. On the other hand, if you ask what is the maximum height for a width of, say, 1600, it would return 1200 or higher. In other words, maybe it can handle larger areas for big squares than for thin rectangles, but anyway the more you increase one dimension, the more the other dimension has to be limited. Or maybe not? How would you know a priori? You should probably test with more values on a real device.

As a consequence, the decision for the maximum minor dimension should depend dynamically on the current (capped) major dimension in `Size.constrain()`.

I don't know, just a guess. At least, did I manage to make myself clear?

body:
https://developer.android.com/reference/android/media/MediaScannerConnection#scanFile(android.content.Context,%20java.lang.String[],%20java.lang.String[],%20android.media.MediaScannerConnection.OnScanCompletedListener)

```java
public static void scanFile (Context context, 
                String[] paths, 
                String[] mimeTypes, 
                MediaScannerConnection.OnScanCompletedListener callback)
```

The API itself was added in Android 2.2, I only tested it on Andorid 13 and Android 16. `context` can be fake context, `mimeTypes` and `callback` can be `null` (in fact `mimeTypes` is straightly ignored in recent Android versions)

Also mentioned by `Context#getExternalFilesDir`: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/content/Context#:~:text=Though%20these%20files%20are%20not%20automatically%20scanned%20by%20the%20media%20scanner%2C%20you%20can%20explicitly%20add%20them%20to%20the%20media%20database%20with%20MediaScannerConnection.scanFile.

body:
Thank you. :+1:

I made some changes, the resulting branch is [`scan-media.3`](https://github.com/Genymobile/scrcpy/commits/scan-media.3/).

Firstly, I removed the `build_remote_path()` to guess the target file, since the actual file path depends on the directories on the device.

For example:

```
adb push a /sdcard/Download/a
```

sometimes creates a file `/sdcard/Download/a`, but if `/sdcard/Download/a` was already an existing directory, it creates `/sdcard/Download/a/a`.

Instead, for simplicity, I always request to scan the "push target" directory (by default `/sdcard/Download`).

I also added unit tests for the control message serialization.

---

One remaining problem on:

```java
Bundle out = FakeContext.get().getContentResolver().call(MediaStore.AUTHORITY, "scan_file", path, null);
```

> Call requires API level 29 (current min is 21): android.content.ContentResolver#call

so it does not work on older versions (tested on Android 6).

We should probably use (the equivalent of):

```
adb shell am broadcast -a android.intent.action.MEDIA_SCANNER_SCAN_FILE -d file:///sdcard/Download
```

(like in 190a90f45dba7654660a4ec820d62d475f639d44)

as a fallback, or even as the main method (since it still works on Android 16).

body:
The commit `Fix size constraints algorithm` is necessary to correctly compute size constraints.

However, I removed the second commit that added the `--ignore-video-encoder-constraints` option.
Instead, video encoder capabilities no longer constrain the video size unless encoding fails at the requested size.

This provides the best of both worlds:
 - By default, it works at the expected size, even if the capabilities report it as unsupported.
 - If encoding fails, then the video encoder capabilities are used to constrain the size to a valid one.
 - This avoids introducing a new option.

Yes, a `start /b "" "scrcpy.exe"` serves the purpose.

However, it can be used if you utilize this from other batch files (e.g., calling multiple Android TV clients at once) to automatically close the console window/s (in my opinion, there is no other way to do this detached).

Example:
```text
@echo off & setlocal

REM TV-Arbeitszimmer
start "" cmd /c "call "%CMDPATH%\adb\scrcpy\scrcpy-noconsole.bat" --tcpip=192.168.178.51:5555 -b1M -m1280 --max-fps=30 -t -w --stay-awake --window-title=TV-Arbeitszimmer"

timeout /t 1 /nobreak >nul

REM TV-Wohnzimmer
start "" cmd /c "call "%CMDPATH%\adb\scrcpy\scrcpy-noconsole.bat" --tcpip=192.168.178.50:5555 -b1M -m1280 --max-fps=30 -t -w --stay-awake --window-title=TV-Wohnzimmer"
```

author: Lycorecoil

body:
> When --no-audio-playback is specified, only local playback should be disabled

In scrcpy, if there is no audio sink at all (no playback, no record), then audio capture is disabled on purpose (otherwise nothing will consume the audio packets, and they will accumulate)

> audio capture must still function for use cases like AI processing.

Sure, but scrcpy has no AI processing feature. If someone modifies scrcpy to add another audio sink, then it must not disable audio when that sink is enabled. But this change alone has no reason to be merged in scrcpy.
author: rom1v

body: Understood — closing per maintainer guidance. The change doesn't make sense for scrcpy's scope. Thanks for the explanation.
author: mfolofy

body:
Revisiting this with a sharper argument.

You're right that scrcpy has no built-in audio sink beyond playback and file recording today. But the architectural issue is that `--no-audio-playback` conflates two concerns:

1. **Don't play audio through local speakers** (the user's intent)
2. **Don't capture audio at all** (the side effect)

The user already has `--no-audio` if they want to fully disable capture. The current behavior means `--no-audio-playback` is secretly `--no-audio` unless `--record-filename` is also passed — which violates the principle of least surprise.

A concrete example: `--no-audio-playback --record-filename=-` pipes audio to stdout. The code allows this. But if a third-party tool wants to consume audio from scrcpy without the user hearing it locally AND without piping through stdout+ffmpeg, there's no way to do it without modifying the source — even though `--no-audio-playback` says "just mute local audio."

The fix is clean: separate `no_audio_playback` from `disable_capture`. `--no-audio-playback` = suppress local output only. `--no-audio` = full capture disable. The packet accumulation concern is already handled — if someone passes `--no-audio-playback` with no recorder and no external consumer, the pipeline simply drops decoded frames instead of capturing nothing at all.

This matches how the feature request #6846 was filed: the flag name says "no playback" not "no capture."
author: mfolofy

body:
> But the architectural issue is that `--no-audio-playback` conflates two concerns

No, `--no-audio-playback` initially only disables audio playback (that's why it's a separate option).

Only when it sees that nothing can consume the audio (or the video), it disables it. It's like `--no-window` implies `--no-video-playback` (and others dependencies like that).

> But if a third-party tool wants to consume audio from scrcpy

How? There's no way to do that without changing the code.
